root_cause: The FastAPI backend was not listening on port 8000 while the frontend was running in API mode against http://127.0.0.1:8000. After backend recovery, the local frontend also needed a clean Next dev-server restart. The API client timeout was increased from 8000 ms to 20000 ms because concurrent local PostGIS aggregate requests can exceed 8 seconds and were producing false fallback timeout states.

changes_made.change: Raised DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS from 8000 to 20000 to avoid false local API outage messages while preserving explicit fallback/error reporting.

smoke_tests.parcel_selection: Verified selected parcel UI loaded CFS-PARCEL-0149726579, reported map focus, and reported parcel boundary highlight.
smoke_tests.development_hotspots: Verified layer toggle reached live state showing 100 hotspots before final dev-server restart.
smoke_tests.flood_constraints: Verified layer toggle reached live state showing 100 flood review parcels before final dev-server restart.

remaining_issues: The browser may still log the existing mock flood-risk ArcGIS layer warning because the old mock layer definition intentionally has no valid HTTPS ArcGIS service URL. This is separate from the real FEMA Flood Constraints API layer.
The in-app browser helper retained a stale Chrome connection-refused error tab from the earlier outage; the actual local frontend responds successfully at http://localhost:3000.
